description_long: This object is a rugged, military-style radio receiver with a robust rectangular body, primarily colored in dark olive green and black. The front panel features a numeric keypad, several rotary knobs, switches, and connection ports, indicating its use for communication and control. A coiled cord connects a classic push-to-talk handset resting on top, and the device is reinforced with metal handles on both sides for portability. The top section includes a boxy module with a label and additional connectors, likely for power or antenna input. The rear and sides are protected by thick, ribbed casing, suggesting durability for field use. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible screws and industrial finishes, suitable for deployment in urban or tactical environments.
description: A rugged, olive green and black radio receiver with a keypad, knobs, and a coiled handset, reinforced by metal handles and a boxy top module. Its robust, utilitarian design suggests use in demanding urban or tactical settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the keypad, control knobs, switches, and connection ports, with the coiled handset resting on top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the boxy top module with a label, visible connectors, and sturdy metal handles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the thick, ribbed casing and the back of the top module, with minimal visible controls.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the coiled cord, the side of the handset, and the robust casing with metal handles.
